rulings.Case Title: ERIC FOSTER VS. BAE SYSTEMS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Eric Foster, Brenda King, Gary Brown, Carlisa Coleman, Eric Dismuke & Barbara Brooks' Motion To Be Relieved From Entry Of Costs On The Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 24, 2014, Line 18, Plaintiffs Eric Foster, Brenda King, Gary Brown, Carlisa Coleman, Eric Dismuke & Barbara Brooks' Motion To Be Relieved From Entry Of Costs On The Judgment. Granted. (CCP § 473(b).) Plaintiffs Eric Foster, Eric Dismuke, Brenda King, Barbara Brooks, Carlisa Coleman and Gary Brown (collectively "Plaintiffs") are relieved from the entry of costs on judgment in favor of Defendants BAE Systems San Francisco Ship Repair Inc., Hugh Vanderspek and Jim Vaught. In its discretion, the Court finds that Farrise Law Firm, P.C., one of the firms representing Plaintiffs, engaged in inadvertence by calendaring in error the deadline to file and serve their motions to tax costs. (Douglas v. Willis (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 287, 290. See Williams Decl., 4.) Plaintiff's counsel Farrise Law Firm, P.C. shall pay reasonable fees and costs in the amount of $ 3,617.60 to Defendants BAE Systems San Francisco Ship Repair Inc., Hugh Vanderspek and Jim Vaught within 30 days of the order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: KARMA QUICK VS. JONATHAN FRIED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 24, 2014, Line 4, CROSS DEFENDANTS KARMA QUICK, DUSTIN COLLIER and DREW TETI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Request for Second Notice is granted. The first and second causes of action in the Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("SACC") arise out of an act in furtherance of Cross-Defendants' right to petition. The SACC alleges that Cross-Defendant Karma Quick provided privileged documents and information to her attorneys, Cross-Defendants Dustin L. Collier and Drew F. Teti, in order for them to prepare for a wage and hour suit against Cross-Plaintiff. (SACC, pars. 44 and 45.) The privileged documents were "reasonably necessary to the preparation and prosecution of the action." (Fox Searchlight Pictures, Inc. v. Paladino (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 294, 314-15.) This is evidenced by the fact that the documents were responsive to a discovery request propounded by Cross-Plaintiff himself. (Declaration of Dustin L. Collier, par. 4.) Although the SACC alleges that Cross-Defendants also provided privileged documents to third-parties outside of the litigation context, "[t]he pleading of other¿ indisputably, unprotected theories of liability does not eliminate or reduce the chilling effect on the exercise of free speech and petition." (Haight Ashbury Free Clinics, Inc. v. Happening House Ventures (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539, 1551.) Thus, Cross-Defendants have met their burden of making a threshold showing that the first and second causes of action arise from protected activity. Cross-Plaintiff has not met his burden of showing a probability of prevailing on the causes of action. Cross-Plaintiff has not submitted any evidence in support of his opposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW V. SMITH VS. THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To Amend The Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 24, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF ANDREW SMITH'S Motion For Leave To Amend The Second Amended Complaint. Denied. California Rule of Court 3.1324(b) provides that a declaration must accompany a motion for leave to amend. CCP sec. 2105.5 provides that a declaration must either state that the declaration was executed in California or state that the declaration was made under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California. This statement is mandatory in order for a declaration to be admitted into evidence. (Kulshrestha v. First Union Commercial Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, 611-12.) Plaintiff's declaration fails to comply with CCP sec. 2105.5. Because the declaration is inadmissible, Plaintiff's motion does not comply with CRC 3.1324(b) and is denied. All requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: ERIN COLLINS; INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHAL OF OTHER VS. THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Deft To Provide Further Responses To Pltf'S Form Interrogatory-General (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 24, 2014, line 1, PLAINTIFF ERIN COLLINS; INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHAL OF OTHER Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Deft To Provide Further Responses To Pltf'S Form Interrogatory-General (Set One) Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF'S MOTIONS TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO CERTAIN FORM INTERROGATORIES, SETS ONE AND TWO OF ITS SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND SETS ONE AND TWO OF ITS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ARE DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE MAJORITY OF DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DISCOVERY ARE WITHOUT MERIT, THE COURT IS OF THE VIEW THAT GIVEN THE TENTATIVE SETTLEMENT IN HARMON V.GAP, NO. RIC 1206620 THE COURT SHOULD NOT REQUIRE DEFENDANT TO MAKE FURTHER DISCOVERY UNTIL THE CONCLUSION OF THAT SETTLEMENT. IT IS CLEAR FROM THE PLEADINGS IN BOTH CASES THAT PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION IS WITHIN THE CLASS COVERED BY THE PLEADINGS IN HARMON AND THAT THE CLASS PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO REPRESENT IN THIS CASE AS WELL AS THE ALLEGED WRONGS SOUGHT TO BE REMEDIED ARE ESSENTIALLY THE SAME CLASS AND ESSENTIALLY THE SAME WRONGS AS ASSERTED IN HARMON. IT WOULD BE A WASTE OF THE PARTIES RESOURCES AND OF THE COURT'S JUDICIAL EFFORTS TO REQUIRE FURTHER DISCOVERY AT THIS POINT. WHEN THE SETTLEMENT IN HARMON IS FINALIZED, PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION CAN EITHER ACCEPT THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT OR OPT OUT OF ANY SETTLEMENT AND PROCEED IN THIS ACTION SEEKING TO REPRESENT HERSELF AND TO REPRESENT ALL OTHER ALLEGED CLASS MEMBERS WHO MAY SIMILARLY OPT OUT OF THE HARMON SETTLEMENT. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =302/jpt

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: ERIN COLLINS; INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHAL OF OTHER VS. THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Deft To Provide Further Responses To Pltf'S Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 24, 2014, line 1, PLAINTIFF ERIN COLLINS; INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHAL OF OTHER Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Deft To Provide Further Responses To Pltf'S Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF'S MOTIONS TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO CERTAIN FORM INTERROGATORIES, SETS ONE AND TWO OF ITS SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND SETS ONE AND TWO OF ITS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ARE DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE MAJORITY OF DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DISCOVERY ARE WITHOUT MERIT, THE COURT IS OF THE VIEW THAT GIVEN THE TENTATIVE SETTLEMENT IN HARMON V.GAP, NO. RIC 1206620 THE COURT SHOULD NOT REQUIRE DEFENDANT TO MAKE FURTHER DISCOVERY UNTIL THE CONCLUSION OF THAT SETTLEMENT. IT IS CLEAR FROM THE PLEADINGS IN BOTH CASES THAT PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION IS WITHIN THE CLASS COVERED BY THE PLEADINGS IN HARMON AND THAT THE CLASS PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO REPRESENT IN THIS CASE AS WELL AS THE ALLEGED WRONGS SOUGHT TO BE REMEDIED ARE ESSENTIALLY THE SAME CLASS AND ESSENTIALLY THE SAME WRONGS AS ASSERTED IN HARMON. IT WOULD BE A WASTE OF THE PARTIES RESOURCES AND OF THE COURT'S JUDICIAL EFFORTS TO REQUIRE FURTHER DISCOVERY AT THIS POINT. WHEN THE SETTLEMENT IN HARMON IS FINALIZED, PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION CAN EITHER ACCEPT THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT OR OPT OUT OF ANY SETTLEMENT AND PROCEED IN THIS ACTION SEEKING TO REPRESENT HERSELF AND TO REPRESENT ALL OTHER ALLEGED CLASS MEMBERS WHO MAY SIMILARLY OPT OUT OF THE HARMON SETTLEMENT. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON MONDAY, MARCH 24, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. DONNA BANKS-JACKSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 24, 2014, Line 15, PLAINTIFF CACH, LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Granted, with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA RETTGER VS. REGENTS OF UNIV OF CA AT SF MED CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Paintiff Sandra Rettiger'S Motion To Amend Pleading
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 24, 2014, Line 22, PLAINTIFF SANDRA RETTIGER'S Motion To Amend Pleading. Denied. Matter was continued on March 18, 2014 to give Plaintiff an opportunity to comply with local rule 2.6b and deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302 by noon on March 18, 2014. Plaintiff was warned that failure to do so would result in the motion being denied. Courtesy copies have not been received in Department 302 and therefore the motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
